turns.user:
Using KoreDocs MCP tools: create a koredoc file in folder /Tests/UpdateTests with filename update-basic-test.koredoc and content exactly: # Initial Version

This is the original content. Version 1.

Using the KoreDocs tool koredocs_update_file, update that file (use the id returned in the previous step) with the following complete replacement content: # Updated Version

This is the revised content. Version 2. Confirm the update succeeded and report the new revision number.
Read back the file using koredocs_get_file or koredocs_list_files for folder /Tests/UpdateTests and confirm the content shows 'Version 2' and not 'Version 1'.
Using KoreDocs MCP: create a koredoc in folder /Tests/UpdateTests, filename replace-test.koredoc, content: ORIGINAL_UNIQUE_STRING_ABC123
Using koredocs_update_file with that file's id, replace the entire content with: REPLACEMENT_UNIQUE_STRING_XYZ789. Confirm success.
Now read that file back using the id and confirm: does the content now contain REPLACEMENT_UNIQUE_STRING_XYZ789? Does it still contain ORIGINAL_UNIQUE_STRING_ABC123?
Using KoreDocs MCP: create a koredoc in folder /Tests/UpdateTests, filename revision-check.koredoc, content: Revision check document - initial.
Using koredocs_update_file: update that file with content 'Revision check document - updated.' Pass expected_revision=1 (the initial revision). Report success or failure and the new revision number.

Using KoreDocs MCP: create a koredoc in folder /Tests/UpdateTests, filename frontmatter-test.koredoc, with content:
---
title: Original Title
tags: test
---

# Original Title

Original body text.

Using koredocs_update_file with that file's id, replace the content with:
---
title: Updated Title
tags: test, updated
---

# Updated Title

Revised body text with new information. Confirm the update was accepted.
Read the file back and confirm the title in the frontmatter is now 'Updated Title' and not 'Original Title'.
Using KoreDocs MCP tools: list all files in folder /Tests/UpdateTests. For each file found, delete it using koredocs_delete_file with its id. Report how many files were deleted.
